Logistics Management

Program Outcomes

MENU

Program Outcomes

1) Explains the basic knowledge of the international logistics field, including the development process of the logistics discipline, new and decisive concepts, scientific methodology, theories and models.

2) Uses appropriate tools and analytical techniques to collect and analyze quantitative and qualitative data in relevant fields, interprets the results and suggests solutions.

3) Designs a logistics system that will support the business mission and objectives.

4) Identifies uncertainties in logistics and supply chain processes and takes measures to eliminate risks.

5) Can discover and create entrepreneurial opportunities and experience to successfully establish and develop one's own business.

6) Analyzes complex and rapidly changing conditions, the dynamics of the national/international business world, and the relevant international logistics and supply chain environment.

7) Can translate and explain the content of documents written in English related to the field.

8) Communicates verbally and in writing in English with people from different social backgrounds, has English proficiency in following and interpreting global dynamics in logistics and supply chain fields.

9) Can set personal, professional, social and business ethical standards, evaluate the ethical dimensions of various practices in relevant fields and be aware of the importance of ethical behavior in the process of adding value to society.
